tour_guide.text: Aldous opened the basement door at the same time Mara was making an offering to the nameless one. One guest has not stopped muttering; their friend is threatening to call the police.
The tour was charming until Aldous answered a question about the 'blood room' with complete honesty. Two guests laughed; one went very quiet.
Aldous forgot three stops but improvised so well a guest asked if the wrong turn was 'part of the liminal architecture.' Vibes are weirdly up.

kitchen.text: Aldous substituted corn starch for corn syrup and the ones who refused to eat are in a much better position than those who didn't.
Breakfast arrived late because Aldous explained the recipe to the toast. It tastes fine if you do not ask what is in it.
Against all odds...this brunch slaps? Aldous insists the measuring cup 'guided him.'

housekeeping.text: Aldous knocked over a crystal lamp which caused a minor explosion. Nothing was *fully* consumed by flame, but the guests don't seem to be taking that well.
Rooms are clean-ish. Aldous left a handwritten note on each pillow apologizing for thought crimes.
Everything sparkles. Aldous also sorted the minibar by emotional resonance. Guests find it upsettingly uplifting without really knowing why.

ritual.text: The ritual became a Q and A about Aldous's dreams. A guest filmed it thinking it was performance art. It is now on someone's story.
Aldous confused the closing chant with a lullaby. Oddly soothing. Authenticity is debatable; vibes are stable.
Aldous accidentally delivered a moving sermon about impermanence. Guests cried. No one is sure what tradition this was.
